metadata.description: AI / ML infra investors. Thesis notes populated from primary sources (recon 2026-07-07) so fund-criteria can tag them.
metadata.default_keywords: AI, machine learning, infrastructure, applied AI, foundation models

watchlist.thesis_alignment: AI across infrastructure and the application layer.
watchlist.notes: Backs AI across foundation-model infrastructure and the application layer. Thesis that every company becomes an AI company; AI-native workflows reimagine software.

watchlist.thesis_alignment: Applied AI and rapid scaling, technical founders.
watchlist.notes: AI angel and investor, early to the generative wave (Perplexity, Harvey, Character). Backs applied AI where AI is essential to the product, and values technical founders who can scale fast.

watchlist.thesis_alignment: Data-driven AI and data infrastructure, early stage.
watchlist.notes: Data-driven thesis. Backs AI and data infrastructure where a proprietary data flywheel is the moat. Early stage.

watchlist.thesis_alignment: AI-native application layer; sells work outcomes, not software.
watchlist.notes: AI thesis: the next trillion-dollar company sells work outcomes, not software tools (services as the new software, the autopilot thesis). Values a proprietary data flywheel tied to a business metric and deep domain expertise, starting from the customer problem rather than technology-first.

watchlist.thesis_alignment: AI-first companies at pre-seed and seed, where AI is essential to the product.
watchlist.notes: AI-first fund ($232M Fund III, solo GP). Backs companies where AI is essential to product functionality and enables entirely new products, not the status quo. Pre-seed and seed, $0.5M-$15M. Focus: AI-first software, developer tools and infrastructure, techbio, defense and security. Stays close to technical direction from day one.

watchlist.thesis_alignment: 'AI eats software'; pre-seed to Series A AI-driven startups.
watchlist.notes: Core thesis 'AI eats software': over the next decade virtually all software is replaced by AI. Pre-seed, seed and Series A ($2-10M focus). AI infrastructure and developer tools, AI for biotech and health, climate and materials, robotics and autonomy. Portfolio: Cohere, Waabi.

watchlist.thesis_alignment: Enterprise AI across infrastructure and applications.
watchlist.notes: Backs enterprise AI across infrastructure and the application layer, early to growth stage.

watchlist.thesis_alignment: Growth-stage AI-enabled software with strong metrics.
watchlist.notes: ScaleUp investor. Backs growth-stage AI-enabled software; values quantified efficiency and revenue metrics.

watchlist.thesis_alignment: Contrarian, ambitious technical founders building transformative tech.
watchlist.notes: Contrarian fund backing ambitious technical founders building transformative deep tech and AI.
SaaS-focused voice and fund. Backs AI-in-SaaS and values quantified revenue, retention and efficiency metrics and product-led growth.

watchlist.thesis_alignment: Early-stage marketplaces and network effects, not AI-exclusive.
watchlist.notes: Benchmark GP. Marketplaces and network-effects thesis, early stage; tracked here for AI-adjacent coverage rather than a pure AI mandate.
